title: Traffic-window and neighbor-pressure features instead of only field averages
summary: Official F1 strategy writeups emphasize clean air, traffic, pit-loss windows, box-to-overtake calls, and whether a driver is in undercut range.
rationale: Official F1 strategy writeups emphasize clean air, traffic, pit-loss windows, box-to-overtake calls, and whether a driver is in undercut range. The current best code uses field means/percentiles, but not local competitor context around the car. A more tactical neighborhood view is closer to how pit calls are actually made.
implementation_hint: Within each `Year-Race-LapNumber`, sort by `Position` and build features from the nearest 1-3 cars ahead/behind: their tyre life, compound, lap delta, recent pit flags, and relative old-tyre pressure. Add counters such as 'neighbors likely to pit now', 'driver at risk of undercut by trailing car', and 'clear-air opportunity if pitting'.
expected_effect: Adds racecraft context that can separate identical tyre states with different tactical surroundings.
risk: Without true time gaps, position-neighbor proxies may be noisy on spread-out fields or during atypical race phases.
